text.NRSV: And the priest, the descendant of Aaron, shall be with the Levites when the Levites receive the tithes; and the Levites shall bring up a tithe of the tithes to the house of our God, to the chambers of the storehouse.
text.Rotherham: And the priest the son of Aaron should be with the Levites, when the Levites should take the tithes,—and the Levites, should bring up the tithe of the tithe, unto the house of our God, into the chambers pertaining unto the treasure-house.
text.KJV: And the priest the son of Aaron shall be with the Levites, when the Levites take tithes: and the Levites shall bring up the tithe of the tithes unto the house of our God, to the chambers, into the treasure house.
text.NABRE: The first batch of our dough, and our offerings of the fruit of every tree, of wine and oil, we will bring to the priests, to the chambers of the house of our God. The tithe of our fields we will bring to the Levites; they, the Levites, shall take the tithe in all the cities of our service.
text.DRB: And the Priest the son of Aaron shall be with the Levites in the tithes of the Levites, and the Levites shall offer the tenth part of their tithe in the house of our God, to the treasury in the treasure-house.

text.NRSV: For the people of Israel and the sons of Levi shall bring the contribution of grain, wine, and oil to the storerooms where the vessels of the sanctuary are, and where the priests that minister, and the gatekeepers and the singers are. We will not neglect the house of our God.
text.Rotherham: For, into the chambers, should the sons of Israel and the sons of Levi bring in the heave-offering of the corn, the new wine and the oil, since, there, are the utensils of the sanctuary, and the priests who are in attendance, and the doorkeepers, and the singers,—so would we not neglect the house of our God.
text.KJV: For the children of Israel and the children of Levi shall bring the offering of the corn, of the new wine, and the oil, unto the chambers, where are the vessels of the sanctuary, and the priests that minister, and the porters, and the singers: and we will not forsake the house of our God.
text.NABRE: An Aaronite priest shall be with the Levites when they take the tithe, and the Levites shall bring the tithe of the tithes to the house of our God, to the chambers of the treasury.
text.DRB: For the children of Israel and the children of Levi shall carry to the treasury the first fruits of corn, of wine, and of oil: and the sanctified vessels shall be there, and the Priests, and singing men, and porters, and ministers, and we will not leave the house of our God.

text.NRSV: Now the leaders of the people lived in Jerusalem; and the rest of the people cast lots to bring one out of ten to live in the holy city Jerusalem, while nine-tenths remained in the other towns.
text.Rotherham: And the rulers of the people dwelt in Jerusalem,—and, the rest of the people, cast lots, to bring in one out of ten to dwell in Jerusalem, the holy city, and nine parts in [other] cities.
text.KJV: And the rulers of the people dwelt at Jerusalem: the rest of the people also cast lots, to bring one of ten to dwell in Jerusalem the holy city, and nine parts to dwell in other cities.
text.NABRE: III. Dedication of the Wall; Other Reforms Chapter 11 Resettlement of Jerusalem. The administrators took up residence in Jerusalem, and the rest of the people cast lots to bring one man in ten to reside in Jerusalem, the holy city, while the other nine would remain in the other cities.
text.DRB: And the Princes of the people dwelt in Jerusalem: but the rest of the people cast lots, to take one part of ten that should dwell in Jerusalem the holy city, and nine parts in the cities.

text.NRSV: And the people blessed all those who willingly offered to live in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And the people bestowed a blessing on all the men, who willingly offered themselves to dwell in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: And the people blessed all the men, that willingly offered themselves to dwell at Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: The people blessed all those who willingly agreed to take up residence in Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And the people blessed all the men that had voluntarily offered themselves to dwell in Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: These are the leaders of the province who lived in Jerusalem; but in the towns of Judah all lived on their property in their towns: Israel, the priests, the Levites, the temple servants, and the descendants of Solomon’s servants.
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the chiefs of the province, who dwelt in Jerusalem, but, in the cities of Judah, dwelt every man in his possession throughout their cities, Israel, the priests, and the Levites and the Nethinim, and the Sons of the Servants of Solomon.
text.KJV: Now these are the chief of the province that dwelt in Jerusalem: but in the cities of Judah dwelt every one in his possession in their cities, to wit, Israel, the priests, and the Levites, and the Nethinims, and the children of Solomon’s servants.
text.NABRE: These are the heads of the province who took up residence in Jerusalem. In the cities of Judah dwelt Israelites, priests, Levites, temple servants, and the descendants of Solomon’s servants, each on the property they owned in their own cities.
text.DRB: These therefore are the Princes of the Province, which dwelt in Jerusalem, and in the cities of Juda. And every one dwelt in his possession, in their cities, Israel, the Priests, the Levites, the Nathineites, and the children of the servants of Salomon.

text.NRSV: And in Jerusalem lived some of the Judahites and of the Benjaminites. Of the Judahites: Athaiah son of Uzziah son of Zechariah son of Amariah son of Shephatiah son of Mahalalel, of the descendants of Perez;
text.Rotherham: And, in Jerusalem, dwelt certain of the sons of Judah and of the sons of Benjamin,—Of the sons of Judah, Athaiah son of Uzziah, son of Zechariah, son of Amariah, son of Shephatiah, son of Mahalalel, of the sons of Perez;
text.KJV: And at Jerusalem dwelt certain of the children of Judah, and of the children of Benjamin. Of the children of Judah; Athaiah the son of Uzziah, the son of Zechariah, the son of Amariah, the son of Shephatiah, the son of Mahalaleel, of the children of Perez;
text.NABRE: In Jerusalem dwelt both Judahites and Benjaminites. Of the Judahites: Athaiah, son of Uzziah, son of Zechariah, son of Amariah, son of Shephatiah, son of Mehallalel, of the sons of Perez;
text.DRB: And in Jerusalem there dwelt of the children of Juda, and of the children of Benjamin: of the children of Juda, Athaias the son of Aziam, the son of Zacharias, the son of Amarias, the son of Saphatias, the son of Malaleel: of the children of Phares,

text.NRSV: and Maaseiah son of Baruch son of Col-hozeh son of Hazaiah son of Adaiah son of Joiarib son of Zechariah son of the Shilonite.
text.Rotherham: and Maaseiah son of Baruch, son of Col-hozeh, son of Hazaiah, son of Adaiah, son of Joiarib, son of Zechariah, son of the Shilonite.
text.KJV: And Maaseiah the son of Baruch, the son of Col–hozeh, the son of Hazaiah, the son of Adaiah, the son of Joiarib, the son of Zechariah, the son of Shiloni.
text.NABRE: Maaseiah, son of Baruch, son of Colhozeh, son of Hazaiah, son of Adaiah, son of Joiarib, son of Zechariah, a son of the Shelanites.
text.DRB: Maasia the son of Baruch, the son of Cholhoza, the son of Hazia, the son of Adaia, the son of Joiarib, the son of Zacharias, the son of a Silonite.

text.NRSV: All the descendants of Perez who lived in Jerusalem were four hundred sixty-eight valiant warriors.
text.Rotherham: All the sons of Perez who were dwelling in Jerusalem, four hundred and sixty-eight, men of ability.
text.KJV: All the sons of Perez that dwelt at Jerusalem were four hundred threescore and eight valiant men.
text.NABRE: The total of the descendants of Perez who dwelt in Jerusalem was four hundred and sixty-eight people of substance.
text.DRB: All these the children of Phares, which dwelt in Jerusalem, four hundred sixty eight, valiant men.

text.NRSV: And these are the Benjaminites: Sallu son of Meshullam son of Joed son of Pedaiah son of Kolaiah son of Maaseiah son of Ithiel son of Jeshaiah.
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the sons of Benjamin,—Sallu son of Meshullam, son of Joed, son of Pedaiah, son of Kolaiah, son of Maaseiah, son of Ithiel, son of Jeshaiah;
text.KJV: And these are the sons of Benjamin; Sallu the son of Meshullam, the son of Joed, the son of Pedaiah, the son of Kolaiah, the son of Maaseiah, the son of Ithiel, the son of Jesaiah.
text.NABRE: These were the Benjaminites: Sallu, son of Meshullam, son of Joed, son of Pedaiah, son of Kolaiah, son of Maaseiah, son of Ithiel, son of Jeshaiah,
text.DRB: And these are the children of Benjamin: Sellum the son of Mosollam, the son of Ioed, the son of Phadaia, the son of Colaia, the son of Masia, the son of Etheel, the son of Isaia,

text.NRSV: And his brothers Gabbai, Sallai: nine hundred twenty-eight.
text.Rotherham: and, after him, Gabbai, Sallai,—nine hundred and twenty-eight.
text.KJV: And after him Gabbai, Sallai, nine hundred twenty and eight.
text.NABRE: and his kinsmen, warriors, nine hundred and twenty-eight in number.
text.DRB: and after him Gebbai, Sellai, nine hundred twenty eight.

text.NRSV: Joel son of Zichri was their overseer; and Judah son of Hassenuah was second in charge of the city.
text.Rotherham: And, Joel son of Zichri, was in charge over them,—and, Judah son of Hassenuah, was over the city, as second.
text.KJV: And Joel the son of Zichri was their overseer: and Judah the son of Senuah was second over the city.
text.NABRE: Joel, son of Zichri, was their commander, and Judah, son of Hassenuah, was second in command of the city.
text.DRB: and Joel the son of Zechri the overseer of them, and Judas the son of Senua second over the city.

text.NRSV: Of the priests: Jedaiah son of Joiarib, Jachin,
text.Rotherham: Of the priests, Jedaiah son of Joiarib, Jachin;
text.KJV: Of the priests: Jedaiah the son of Joiarib, Jachin.
text.NABRE: Among the priests were: Jedaiah; Joiarib; Jachin;
text.DRB: And of the Priests, Idaia the son of Joarib, Jachin,

text.NRSV: Seraiah son of Hilkiah son of Meshullam son of Zadok son of Meraioth son of Ahitub, officer of the house of God,
text.Rotherham: Seraiah son of Hilkiah, son of Meshullam, son of Zadok, son of Meraioth, son of Ahitub, chief ruler of the house of God;
text.KJV: Seraiah the son of Hilkiah, the son of Meshullam, the son of Zadok, the son of Meraioth, the son of Ahitub, was the ruler of the house of God.
text.NABRE: Seraiah, son of Hilkiah, son of Meshullam, son of Zadok, son of Meraioth, son of Ahitub, the ruler of the house of God,
text.DRB: Saraia the son of Helcias, the son of Mosollam, the son of Sadoc, the son of Meraioth, the son of Achitob the Prince of the house of God,

text.NRSV: and their associates who did the work of the house, eight hundred twenty-two; and Adaiah son of Jeroham son of Pelaliah son of Amzi son of Zechariah son of Pashhur son of Malchijah,
text.Rotherham: and their brethren who were doing the work of the house, eight hundred and twenty-two,—and Adaiah son of Jeroham, son of Pelaliah, son of Amzi, son of Zechariah, son of Pashhur, son of Malchijah;
text.KJV: And their brethren that did the work of the house were eight hundred twenty and two: and Adaiah the son of Jeroham, the son of Pelaliah, the son of Amzi, the son of Zechariah, the son of Pashur, the son of Malchiah,
text.NABRE: and their kinsmen who carried out the temple service, eight hundred and twenty-two; Adaiah, son of Jeroham, son of Pelaliah, son of Amzi, son of Zechariah, son of Pashhur, son of Malchijah,
text.DRB: and their brethren that do the works of the temple: eight hundred twenty two. And Adaia the son of Jeroham, the son of Phelelia, the son of Amsi, the son of Zacharias, the son of Pheshur, the son of Melchias,

text.NRSV: and his associates, heads of ancestral houses, two hundred forty-two; and Amashsai son of Azarel son of Ahzai son of Meshillemoth son of Immer,
text.Rotherham: and his brethren, ancestral chiefs, two hundred and forty-two,—and Amashsai son of Azarel, son of Ahzai, son of Meshillemoth, son of Immer;
text.KJV: And his brethren, chief of the fathers, two hundred forty and two: and Amashai the son of Azareel, the son of Ahasai, the son of Meshillemoth, the son of Immer,
text.NABRE: and his kinsmen, heads of ancestral houses, two hundred and forty-two; and Amasai, son of Azarel, son of Ahzai, son of Meshillemoth, son of Immer,
text.DRB: and his brethren the Princes of the fathers: two hundred forty two. And Amassai the son of Azreel, the son of Ahazi, the son of Mosolamoth, the son of Emmer,

text.NRSV: and their associates, valiant warriors, one hundred twenty-eight; their overseer was Zabdiel son of Haggedolim.
text.Rotherham: and their brethren, strong men of ability, a hundred and twenty-eight,—and, he who was in charge of them, was Zabdiel, son of Haggedolim.
text.KJV: And their brethren, mighty men of valour, an hundred twenty and eight: and their overseer was Zabdiel, the son of one of the great men.
text.NABRE: and his kinsmen, warriors, one hundred and twenty-eight. Their commander was Zabdiel, son of Haggadol.
text.DRB: and their brethren exceeding mighty: an hundred twenty eight, and their overseer Zabdiel son of the mighty ones.

text.NRSV: And of the Levites: Shemaiah son of Hasshub son of Azrikam son of Hashabiah son of Bunni;
text.Rotherham: And, of the Levites, Shemaiah son of Hasshub, son of Azrikam, son of Hashabiah, son of Bunni;
text.KJV: Also of the Levites: Shemaiah the son of Hashub, the son of Azrikam, the son of Hashabiah, the son of Bunni;
text.NABRE: Among the Levites were Shemaiah, son of Hasshub, son of Azrikam, son of Hashabiah, son of Bunni;
text.DRB: And of the Levites Semeia the son of Hasub, the son of Azaricam, the son of Hasabia, the son of Boni,

text.NRSV: and Shabbethai and Jozabad, of the leaders of the Levites, who were over the outside work of the house of God;
text.Rotherham: and Shabbethai and Jozabad, over the outside business of the house of God, of the chiefs of the Levites;
text.KJV: And Shabbethai and Jozabad, of the chief of the Levites, had the oversight of the outward business of the house of God.
text.NABRE: Shabbethai and Jozabad, levitical chiefs who were placed over the external affairs of the house of God;
text.DRB: and Sabathai and Jozabed, over all the works, that were without the house of God, of the Princes of the Levites.

text.NRSV: and Mattaniah son of Mica son of Zabdi son of Asaph, who was the leader to begin the thanksgiving in prayer, and Bakbukiah, the second among his associates; and Abda son of Shammua son of Galal son of Jeduthun.
text.Rotherham: and Mattaniah son of Mica, son of Zabdi, son of Asaph, leader of the praise who giveth thanks, in prayer, and Bakbukiah the second among his brethren,—and Obadiah son of Shemaiah, son of Galal, son of Jeduthun.
text.KJV: And Mattaniah the son of Micha, the son of Zabdi, the son of Asaph, was the principal to begin the thanksgiving in prayer: and Bakbukiah the second among his brethren, and Abda the son of Shammua, the son of Galal, the son of Jeduthun.
text.NABRE: Mattaniah, son of Micah, son of Zabdi, son of Asaph, director of the psalms, who led the thanksgiving at prayer; Bakbukiah, second in rank among his kinsmen; and Abda, son of Shammua, son of Galal, son of Jeduthun.
text.DRB: And Mathania the son of Micha, the son of Zebedei, the son of Asaph Prince to praise, and to confess in prayer, and Becbecia second of his brethren, and Abda the son of Samua, the son of Galal, the son of Idithun.

text.NRSV: All the Levites in the holy city were two hundred eighty-four.
text.Rotherham: All the Levites in the holy city, were two hundred and eighty-four.
text.KJV: All the Levites in the holy city were two hundred fourscore and four.
text.NABRE: The total of the Levites in the holy city was two hundred and eighty-four.
text.DRB: All the Levites in the holy city two hundred eighty four.

text.NRSV: The gatekeepers, Akkub, Talmon and their associates, who kept watch at the gates, were one hundred seventy-two.
text.Rotherham: And, the door-keepers—Akkub, Talmon, and their brethren who were keeping watch in the gates, were a hundred and seventy-two.
text.KJV: Moreover the porters, Akkub, Talmon, and their brethren that kept the gates, were an hundred seventy and two.
text.NABRE: The gatekeepers were Akkub, Talmon, and their kinsmen, who kept watch over the gates; one hundred and seventy-two in number.
text.DRB: And the porters, Accub, Telmon, and their brethren, which kept the doors: an hundred seventy two.

text.NRSV: And the rest of Israel, and of the priests and the Levites, were in all the towns of Judah, all of them in their inheritance.
text.Rotherham: But, the residue of Israel, the priests, the Levites, were in all the cities of Judah, every one in his inheritance.
text.KJV: And the residue of Israel, of the priests, and the Levites, were in all the cities of Judah, every one in his inheritance.
text.NABRE: The rest of Israel, including priests and Levites, were in all the other cities of Judah in their own inheritances.
text.DRB: And the rest of Israel the Priests and the Levites in all the cities of Juda, every man in his possession.

text.NRSV: But the temple servants lived on Ophel; and Ziha and Gishpa were over the temple servants.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, the Nethinim, were dwelling in Ophel,—and, Ziha and Gishpa, were over the Nethinim.
text.KJV: But the Nethinims dwelt in Ophel: and Ziha and Gispa were over the Nethinims.
text.NABRE: The temple servants lived on Ophel. Ziha and Gishpa were in charge of the temple servants.
text.DRB: And the Nathineites, that dwelt in Ophel, and Siaha, and Gaspha of the Nathineites.

text.NRSV: The overseer of the Levites in Jerusalem was Uzzi son of Bani son of Hashabiah son of Mattaniah son of Mica, of the descendants of Asaph, the singers, in charge of the work of the house of God.
text.Rotherham: And, the overseer of the Levites in Jerusalem, was Uzzi son of Bani, son of Hashabiah, son of Mattaniah, son of Mica,—of the sons of Asaph the singers, to take lead in the business of the house of God.
text.KJV: The overseer also of the Levites at Jerusalem was Uzzi the son of Bani, the son of Hashabiah, the son of Mattaniah, the son of Micha. Of the sons of Asaph, the singers were over the business of the house of God.
text.NABRE: The prefect of the Levites in Jerusalem was Uzzi, son of Bani, son of Hashabiah, son of Mattaniah, son of Micah; he was one of the descendants of Asaph, the singers appointed to the service of the house of God—
text.DRB: And the overseer of the Levites in Jerusalem, Azzi the son of Bani, the son of Hasabia, the son of Mathania, the son of Micha. Of the children of Asaph, the singing men in the ministry of the house of God.

text.NRSV: For there was a command from the king concerning them, and a settled provision for the singers, as was required every day.
text.Rotherham: For, the commandment of the king, was upon them,—as to a fixed provision for the singers, the need of a day upon its day.
text.KJV: For it was the king’s commandment concerning them, that a certain portion should be for the singers, due for every day.
text.NABRE: for they had been appointed by royal decree, and there was a fixed schedule for the singers assigning them their daily duties.
text.DRB: For the king's commandment was upon them, and an order among the singing men day by day.

text.NRSV: And Pethahiah son of Meshezabel, of the descendants of Zerah son of Judah, was at the king’s hand in all matters concerning the people.
text.Rotherham: And, Pethahiah son of Meshezabel, of the sons of Zerah, son of Judah, was at the hand of the king, in every matter pertaining to the people.
text.KJV: And Pethahiah the son of Meshezabeel, of the children of Zerah the son of Judah, was at the king’s hand in all matters concerning the people.
text.NABRE: Pethahiah, son of Meshezabel, a descendant of Zerah, son of Judah, was royal deputy in all affairs that concerned the people.
text.DRB: And Phathahia the son of Mesezebel of the children of Zara the son of Juda in the hand of the king, according to every word of the people,

text.NRSV: And as for the villages, with their fields, some of the people of Judah lived in Kiriath-arba and its villages, and in Dibon and its villages, and in Jekabzeel and its villages,
text.Rotherham: And, as for the villages in their fields, some of the sons of Judah, dwelt in Kiriath-arba, and the hamlets thereof, and in Dibon, and the hamlets thereof, and in Jekabzeel, and the villages thereof;
text.KJV: And for the villages, with their fields, some of the children of Judah dwelt at Kirjath–arba, and in the villages thereof, and at Dibon, and in the villages thereof, and at Jekabzeel, and in the villages thereof,
text.NABRE: Other Settlements. As concerns their villages with their fields: Judahites lived in Kiriath-arba and its dependencies, in Dibon and its dependencies, in Jekabzeel and its villages,
text.DRB: and in the houses through all their countries. Of the children of Juda there dwelt in Cariatharbe, and in her daughters: and in Dibon, and in her daughters, and in Cabseel, and in the villages thereof,

text.NRSV: and in Jeshua and in Moladah and Beth-pelet,
text.Rotherham: and in Jeshua, and in Moladah, and in Beth-pelet;
text.KJV: And at Jeshua, and at Moladah, and at Beth–phelet,
text.NABRE: in Jeshua, Moladah, Beth-pelet,
text.DRB: and in Jesue, and in Molada, and in Bethphaleth,

text.NRSV: in Hazar-shual, in Beer-sheba and its villages,
text.Rotherham: and in Hazar-shual, and in Beer-sheba, and the hamlets thereof;
text.KJV: And at Hazar–shual, and at Beer–sheba, and in the villages thereof,
text.NABRE: in Hazarshual, in Beer-sheba and its dependencies,
text.DRB: and in Hasersual, and in Bersabee, and in her daughters,

text.NRSV: in Ziklag, in Meconah and its villages,
text.Rotherham: and in Ziklag, and in Meconah, and in the hamlets thereof,
text.KJV: And at Ziklag, and at Mekonah, and in the villages thereof,
text.NABRE: in Ziklag, in Meconah and its dependencies,
text.DRB: and in Siceleg, and in Mochona, and in her daughters,

text.NRSV: in En-rimmon, in Zorah, in Jarmuth,
text.Rotherham: and in En-rimmmon, and in Zorah, and in Jarmuth;
text.KJV: And at En–rimmon, and at Zareah, and at Jarmuth,
text.NABRE: in En-rimmon, Zorah, Jarmuth,
text.DRB: and in Remmon, and in Saraa, and in Jerimuth,

text.NRSV: Zanoah, Adullam, and their villages, Lachish and its fields, and Azekah and its villages. So they camped from Beer-sheba to the valley of Hinnom.
text.Rotherham: Zanoah, Adullam and their villages, Lachish, and the fields thereof, Azekah, and the hamlets thereof,—so they encamped from Beer-sheba, unto the valley of Hinnom.
text.KJV: Zanoah, Adullam, and in their villages, at Lachish, and the fields thereof, at Azekah, and in the villages thereof. And they dwelt from Beer–sheba unto the valley of Hinnom.
text.NABRE: Zanoah, Adullam, and their villages, Lachish and its fields, Azekah and its dependencies. They were settled from Beer-sheba to Ge-hinnom.
text.DRB: Zanoa, Odollam, and in their towns, Lachis and in her countries, Azeca, and in her daughters. And they abode in Bersabee unto the vale of Ennom.

text.NRSV: The people of Benjamin also lived from Geba onward, at Michmash, Aija, Bethel and its villages,
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Benjamin, [dwelt] from Geba, to Michmas and Aija, and Bethel, and the hamlets thereof:
text.KJV: The children also of Benjamin from Geba dwelt at Michmash, and Aija, and Beth–el, and in their villages,
text.NABRE: Benjaminites were in Geba, Michmash, Aija, Bethel and its dependencies,
text.DRB: And the children of Benjamin, of Geba, Mecmas, and Hai, and Bethel, and her daughters,

text.NRSV: Anathoth, Nob, Ananiah,
text.Rotherham: Anathoth, Nob, Ananiah;
text.KJV: And at Anathoth, Nob, Ananiah,
text.NABRE: Anathoth, Nob, Ananiah,
text.DRB: in Anathoth, Nob, Anania,

text.NRSV: Hazor, Ramah, Gittaim,
text.Rotherham: Hazor, Ramah, Gittaim;
text.KJV: Hazor, Ramah, Gittaim,
text.NABRE: Hazor, Ramah, Gittaim,
text.DRB: Asor, Rama, Gethaim,

text.NRSV: Hadid, Zeboim, Neballat,
text.Rotherham: Hadid, Zeboim, Neballat;
text.KJV: Hadid, Zeboim, Neballat,
text.NABRE: Hadid, Zeboim, Neballat,
text.DRB: Hadid, Seboim, and Neballat, Lod,

text.NRSV: Lod, and Ono, the valley of artisans.
text.Rotherham: Lod and Ono, the valley of craftsmen.
text.KJV: Lod, and Ono, the valley of craftsmen.
text.NABRE: Lod, Ono, and the Valley of the Artisans.
text.DRB: and Ono the valley of artificers.

text.NRSV: And certain divisions of the Levites in Judah were joined to Benjamin.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, of the Levites, certain courses of Judah, pertained unto Benjamin.
text.KJV: And of the Levites were divisions in Judah, and in Benjamin.
text.NABRE: Some divisions of the Levites from Judah were attached to Benjamin.
text.DRB: And of the Levites were portions of Juda, and Benjamin.

text.NRSV: These are the priests and the Levites who came up with Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, and Jeshua: Seraiah, Jeremiah, Ezra,
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the priests and the Levites, who came up with Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, and Jeshua,—Seraiah, Jeremiah, Ezra;
text.KJV: Now these are the priests and the Levites that went up with Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel, and Jeshua: Seraiah, Jeremiah, Ezra,
text.NABRE: Chapter 12 Priests and Levites at the Time of Zerubbabel. The following are the priests and Levites who returned with Zerubbabel, son of Shealtiel, and Jeshua: Seraiah, Jeremiah, Ezra,
text.DRB: And these are the Priests and Levites, that came up with Zorobabel the son of Salathiel, and Josue: Saraia, Jeremias, Esdras,

text.NRSV: Amariah, Malluch, Hattush,
text.Rotherham: Amariah, Malluch, Hattush;
text.KJV: Amariah, Malluch, Hattush,
text.NABRE: Amariah, Malluch, Hattush,
text.DRB: Amaria, Melluch, Hattus.

text.NRSV: Shecaniah, Rehum, Meremoth,
text.Rotherham: Shecaniah, Rehum, Meremoth;
text.KJV: Shechaniah, Rehum, Meremoth,
text.NABRE: Shecaniah, Rehum, Meremoth,
text.DRB: Sebenias, Rheum, Merimuth,

text.NRSV: Iddo, Ginnethoi, Abijah,
text.Rotherham: Iddo, Ginnethoi, Abijah;
text.KJV: Iddo, Ginnetho, Abijah,
text.NABRE: Iddo, Ginnethon, Abijah,
text.DRB: Addo, Genthon, Abia,

text.NRSV: Mijamin, Maadiah, Bilgah,
text.Rotherham: Mijamin, Maadiah, Bilgah;
text.KJV: Miamin, Maadiah, Bilgah,
text.NABRE: Mijamin, Maadiah, Bilgah,
text.DRB: Miamin, Madia, Belga,

text.NRSV: Shemaiah, Joiarib, Jedaiah,
text.Rotherham: Shemaiah, and Joiarib, Jedaiah;
text.KJV: Shemaiah, and Joiarib, Jedaiah,
text.NABRE: Shemaiah, and Joiarib, Jedaiah,
text.DRB: Semeia, and Joiarib, Idaia, Sellum, Amoc, Helcias,

text.NRSV: Sallu, Amok, Hilkiah, Jedaiah. These were the leaders of the priests and of their associates in the days of Jeshua.
text.Rotherham: Sallu, Amok, Hilkiah, Jedaiah,—these, were the chiefs of the priests and their brethren, in the days of Jeshua.
text.KJV: Sallu, Amok, Hilkiah, Jedaiah. These were the chief of the priests and of their brethren in the days of Jeshua.
text.NABRE: Sallu, Amok, Hilkiah, Jedaiah. These were the priestly heads and their kinsmen in the days of Jeshua.
text.DRB: Idaia. These are the Princes of the Priests, & their brethren in the days of Josue.

text.NRSV: And the Levites: Jeshua, Binnui, Kadmiel, Sherebiah, Judah, and Mattaniah, who with his associates was in charge of the songs of thanksgiving.
text.Rotherham: And, the Levites, Jeshua, Binnui, Kadmiel, Sherebiah, Judah, Mattaniah,—over the choirs, he and his brethren;
text.KJV: Moreover the Levites: Jeshua, Binnui, Kadmiel, Sherebiah, Judah, and Mattaniah, which was over the thanksgiving, he and his brethren.
text.NABRE: The Levites were Jeshua, Binnui, Kadmiel, Sherebiah, Judah, Mattaniah, who, together with his kinsmen, was in charge of the thanksgiving hymns,
text.DRB: Moreover the Levites, Jesua, Bennui, Cedmihel, Sarebia, Juda, Mathanias, over the hymns they & their brethren:

text.NRSV: And Bakbukiah and Unno their associates stood opposite them in the service.
text.Rotherham: and Bakbukiah and Unno their brethren, were over against them, in wards.
text.KJV: Also Bakbukiah and Unni, their brethren, were over against them in the watches.
text.NABRE: while Bakbukiah and Unno and their kinsmen ministered opposite them by turns.
text.DRB: And Becbecia, and Hannia and their brethren every one in his office.

text.NRSV: Jeshua was the father of Joiakim, Joiakim the father of Eliashib, Eliashib the father of Joiada,
text.Rotherham: And Jeshua, begat Joiakim,—and Joiakim, begat Eliashib, and, Eliashib, [begat] Joiada;
text.KJV: And Jeshua begat Joiakim, Joiakim also begat Eliashib, and Eliashib begat Joiada,
text.NABRE: High Priests. Jeshua became the father of Joiakim, Joiakim the father of Eliashib, and Eliashib the father of Joiada;
text.DRB: And Josue begat Ioacim, and Ioacim begat Eliasib, and Eliasib begat Joiada,

text.NRSV: Joiada the father of Jonathan, and Jonathan the father of Jaddua.
text.Rotherham: and, Joiada, begat Jonathan,—and, Jonathan, begat Jaddua.
text.KJV: And Joiada begat Jonathan, and Jonathan begat Jaddua.
text.NABRE: Joiada the father of Johanan, and Johanan the father of Jaddua.
text.DRB: and Joiada begat Jonathan, and Jonathan begat Ieddoa.

text.NRSV: In the days of Joiakim the priests, heads of ancestral houses, were: of Seraiah, Meraiah; of Jeremiah, Hananiah;
text.Rotherham: And, in the days of Joiakim, were priests, ancestral chiefs,—of Seraiah, Meraiah, of Jeremiah, Hananiah;
text.KJV: And in the days of Joiakim were priests, the chief of the fathers: of Seraiah, Meraiah; of Jeremiah, Hananiah;
text.NABRE: Priests and Levites Under Joiakim. In the days of Joiakim these were the priestly family heads: for Seraiah, Meraiah; for Jeremiah, Hananiah;
text.DRB: And in the days of Ioacim the Priests and Princes of the families were: Of Saraia, Maraia: Of Jeremias, Hanania:

text.NRSV: of Ezra, Meshullam; of Amariah, Jehohanan;
text.Rotherham: of Ezra, Meshullam,—of Amariah, Jehohanan;
text.KJV: Of Ezra, Meshullam; of Amariah, Jehohanan;
text.NABRE: for Ezra, Meshullam; for Amariah, Jehohanan;
text.DRB: Of Esdras, Mosollam: Of Amaria, Johanan:

text.NRSV: of Malluchi, Jonathan; of Shebaniah, Joseph;
text.Rotherham: of Malluchi, Jonathan,—of Shebaniah, Joseph;
text.KJV: Of Melicu, Jonathan; of Shebaniah, Joseph;
text.NABRE: for Malluchi, Jonathan; for Shebaniah, Joseph;
text.DRB: Of Milicho, Jonathan: Of Sebenia, Joseph:

text.NRSV: of Harim, Adna; of Meraioth, Helkai;
text.Rotherham: of Harim, Adna,—of Meraioth, Helkai;
text.KJV: Of Harim, Adna; of Meraioth, Helkai;
text.NABRE: for Harim, Adna; for Meremoth, Helkai;
text.DRB: Of Haram, Edna: Of Maraioth, Helci:

text.NRSV: of Iddo, Zechariah; of Ginnethon, Meshullam;
text.Rotherham: of Iddo, Zechariah,—of Ginnethon, Meshullam;
text.KJV: Of Iddo, Zechariah; of Ginnethon, Meshullam;
text.NABRE: for Iddo, Zechariah; for Ginnethon, Meshullam;
text.DRB: Of Adaia, Zacharia: Of Genthon, Mosollam:

text.NRSV: of Abijah, Zichri; of Miniamin, of Moadiah, Piltai;
text.Rotherham: of Abijah, Zichri,—of Miniamin, of Moadiah, Piltai;
text.KJV: Of Abijah, Zichri; of Miniamin, of Moadiah, Piltai;
text.NABRE: for Abijah, Zichri; for Miamin,…; for Moadiah, Piltai;
text.DRB: Of Abia, Zechri: Of Miamin and Moadia, Phelti:

text.NRSV: of Bilgah, Shammua; of Shemaiah, Jehonathan;
text.Rotherham: of Bilgah, Shammua,—of Shemaiah, Jehonathan;
text.KJV: Of Bilgah, Shammua; of Shemaiah, Jehonathan;
text.NABRE: for Bilgah, Shammua; for Shemaiah, Jehonathan;
text.DRB: Of Belga, Sammua: Of Semaia, Jonathan:

text.NRSV: of Joiarib, Mattenai; of Jedaiah, Uzzi;
text.Rotherham: and, of Joiarib, Mattenai,—of Jedaiah, Uzzi;
text.KJV: And of Joiarib, Mattenai; of Jedaiah, Uzzi;
text.NABRE: and for Joiarib, Mattenai; for Jedaiah, Uzzi;
text.DRB: Of Joiarib, Mathanai: Of Iodaia, Azzi:

text.NRSV: of Sallai, Kallai; of Amok, Eber;
text.Rotherham: of Sallai, Kallai,—of Amok, Eber;
text.KJV: Of Sallai, Kallai; of Amok, Eber;
text.NABRE: for Sallu, Kallai; for Amok, Eber;
text.DRB: Of Sellai, Celai: Of Amoc, Heber:

text.NRSV: of Hilkiah, Hashabiah; of Jedaiah, Nethanel.
text.Rotherham: of Hilkiah, Hashabiah,—of Jedaiah, Nethanel.
text.KJV: Of Hilkiah, Hashabiah; of Jedaiah, Nethaneel.
text.NABRE: for Hilkiah, Hashabiah; for Jedaiah, Nethanel.
text.DRB: Of Helcias, Hasebia: Of Idaia, Nathanael.

text.NRSV: As for the Levites, in the days of Eliashib, Joiada, Johanan, and Jaddua, there were recorded the heads of ancestral houses; also the priests until the reign of Darius the Persian.
text.Rotherham: The Levites—in the days of Eliashib, Joiada, and Johanan, and Jaddua, were recorded as ancestral chiefs,—also the priests, unto the reign of Darius the Persian.
text.KJV: The Levites in the days of Eliashib, Joiada, and Johanan, and Jaddua, were recorded chief of the fathers: also the priests, to the reign of Darius the Persian.
text.NABRE: In the time of Eliashib, Joiada, Johanan, and Jaddua, the heads of ancestral houses of the priests were written down in the Book of Chronicles, up until the reign of Darius the Persian.
text.DRB: The Levites in the days of Eliasib, and Joiada, and Johanan, and Ieddoa, written Princes of the families, and the Priests in the reign of Darius the Persian.

text.NRSV: The Levites, heads of ancestral houses, were recorded in the Book of the Annals until the days of Johanan son of Eliashib.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Levi, ancestral heads, were written in the book of Chronicles,—even until the days of Johanan, son of Eliashib.
text.KJV: The sons of Levi, the chief of the fathers, were written in the book of the chronicles, even until the days of Johanan the son of Eliashib.
text.NABRE: The sons of Levi: the family heads were written down in the Book of Chronicles, up until the time of Johanan, the son of Eliashib.
text.DRB: The children of Levi, Princes of the families, written in the book of Chronicles of days, and unto the days of Jonathan the son of Eliasib.

text.NRSV: And the leaders of the Levites: Hashabiah, Sherebiah, and Jeshua son of Kadmiel, with their associates over against them, to praise and to give thanks, according to the commandment of David the man of God, section opposite to section.
text.Rotherham: And, the chiefs of the Levites, Hashabiah, Sherebiah, and Jeshua son of Kadmiel, with their brethren over against them, to praise—to give thanks, by the commandment of David the man of God,—ward joined to ward.
text.KJV: And the chief of the Levites: Hashabiah, Sherebiah, and Jeshua the son of Kadmiel, with their brethren over against them, to praise and to give thanks, according to the commandment of David the man of God, ward over against ward.
text.NABRE: The heads of the Levites were Hashabiah, Sherebiah, Jeshua, Binnui, Kadmiel. Their kinsmen who stood opposite them to sing praises and thanksgiving in fulfillment of the command of David, the man of God, one section opposite the other,
text.DRB: And the Princes of the Levites, Hasebia, Serebia, and Josue the son of Cedmihel: and their brethren by their courses, to praise and confess according to the precept of David the man of God, and to wait equally in order.

text.NRSV: Mattaniah, Bakbukiah, Obadiah, Meshullam, Talmon, and Akkub were gatekeepers standing guard at the storehouses of the gates.
text.Rotherham: Mattaniah and Bakbukiah, Obadiah, Meshullam, Talmon, Akkub, were watchers, doorkeepers of the ward, in the storehouses of the gates.
text.KJV: Mattaniah, and Bakbukiah, Obadiah, Meshullam, Talmon, Akkub, were porters keeping the ward at the thresholds of the gates.
text.NABRE: were Mattaniah, Bakbukiah, Obadiah. Meshullam, Talmon, and Akkub were gatekeepers. They guarded the storerooms at the gates.
text.DRB: Mathania, and Becbecia, Obedia, and Mosollam, Telmon, Accub, keepers of the gates and of the entrances before the gates.

text.NRSV: These were in the days of Joiakim son of Jeshua son of Jozadak, and in the days of the governor Nehemiah and of the priest Ezra, the scribe.
text.Rotherham: These, were in the days of Joiakim, son of Jeshua, son of Jozadak,—and in the days of Nehemiah the pasha, and Ezra the priest the scribe.
text.KJV: These were in the days of Joiakim the son of Jeshua, the son of Jozadak, and in the days of Nehemiah the governor, and of Ezra the priest, the scribe.
text.NABRE: All these lived in the time of Joiakim, son of Jeshua, son of Jozadak (and in the time of Nehemiah the governor and of Ezra the priest-scribe).
text.DRB: These were in the days of Ioacim the son of Josue, the son Josedec, and in the days of Nehemias the duke, and of Esdras the Priest, and Scribe.

text.NRSV: Now at the dedication of the wall of Jerusalem they sought out the Levites in all their places, to bring them to Jerusalem to celebrate the dedication with rejoicing, with thanksgivings and with singing, with cymbals, harps, and lyres.
text.Rotherham: And, at the dedication of the wall of Jerusalem, they sought the Levites, out of all their places, to bring them to Jerusalem,—to keep the dedication and the rejoicing, both with thanksgiving and with music, cymbals, harps, and with lyres.
text.KJV: And at the dedication of the wall of Jerusalem they sought the Levites out of all their places, to bring them to Jerusalem, to keep the dedication with gladness, both with thanksgivings, and with singing, with cymbals, psalteries, and with harps.
text.NABRE: Dedication of the Wall. At the dedication of the wall of Jerusalem, the Levites were sought out wherever they lived and were brought to Jerusalem to celebrate a joyful dedication with thanksgiving hymns and the music of cymbals, harps, and lyres.
text.DRB: And in the dedication of the wall of Jerusalem they sought Levites out of all other places, to bring them into Jerusalem, and to make the dedication and joy in giving of thanks, and song, and in cymbals, psalteries and harps.

text.NRSV: The companies of the singers gathered together from the circuit around Jerusalem and from the villages of the Netophathites;
text.Rotherham: So the sons of the singers gathered themselves together,—both out of the circuit round about Jerusalem, and out of the villages of the Netophathites;
text.KJV: And the sons of the singers gathered themselves together, both out of the plain country round about Jerusalem, and from the villages of Netophathi;
text.NABRE: The levitical singers gathered together from the region about Jerusalem, from the villages of the Netophathites,
text.DRB: And the children of the singing men were gathered together out of the champion about Jerusalem, and out of the towns Nethuphati,

text.NRSV: also from Beth-gilgal and from the region of Geba and Azmaveth; for the singers had built for themselves villages around Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: also out of Beth-gilgal, and out of the fields of Geba, and Azmaveth,—for, villages, had the singers builded for themselves, round about Jerusalem.
text.KJV: Also from the house of Gilgal, and out of the fields of Geba and Azmaveth: for the singers had builded them villages round about Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: from Beth-gilgal, and from the plains of Geba and Azmaveth (for the singers had built themselves settlements about Jerusalem).
text.DRB: and from the house of Galgal, and from the countries of Geba and Azmaveth: because the singing men did build themselves villages round about Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: And the priests and the Levites purified themselves; and they purified the people and the gates and the wall.
text.Rotherham: And the priests and the Levites purified themselves,—and purified the people, and the gates, and the wall.
text.KJV: And the priests and the Levites purified themselves, and purified the people, and the gates, and the wall.
text.NABRE: The priests and Levites first purified themselves, then they purified the people, the gates, and the wall.
text.DRB: And the Priests and Levites were cleansed, and they cleansed the people, and the gates, & the wall.

text.NRSV: Then I brought the leaders of Judah up onto the wall, and appointed two great companies that gave thanks and went in procession. One went to the right on the wall to the Dung Gate;
text.Rotherham: Then brought I up the rulers of Judah upon the wall,—and I appointed two large choirs, even to go in procession to the right, upon the wall, towards the dung-gate;
text.KJV: Then I brought up the princes of Judah upon the wall, and appointed two great companies of them that gave thanks, whereof one went on the right hand upon the wall toward the dung gate:
text.NABRE: I had the administrators of Judah go up on the wall, and I arranged two great choirs. The first of these proceeded to the right, along the top of the wall, in the direction of the Dung Gate,
text.DRB: And I made the Princes of Juda go up upon the wall, and I set two great choirs of them that should praise. And they went on the right hand upon the wall to the gate of the dunghill.

text.NRSV: and after them went Hoshaiah and half the officials of Judah,
text.Rotherham: and after them went Hoshaiah, and half the rulers of Judah;
text.KJV: And after them went Hoshaiah, and half of the princes of Judah,
text.NABRE: followed by Hoshaiah and half the administrators of Judah,
text.DRB: And after them went Osaias, & the half part of the Princes of Juda,

text.NRSV: and Azariah, Ezra, Meshullam,
text.Rotherham: then Azariah, Ezra, and Meshullam;
text.KJV: And Azariah, Ezra, and Meshullam,
text.NABRE: along with Azariah, Ezra, Meshullam,
text.DRB: and Azarias, Esdras, & Mosollam, Judas, & Benjamin, & Semeia, & Jeremias.

text.NRSV: Judah, Benjamin, Shemaiah, and Jeremiah,
text.Rotherham: Judah, and Benjamin, and Shemaiah, and Jeremiah;
text.KJV: Judah, and Benjamin, and Shemaiah, and Jeremiah,
text.NABRE: Judah, Benjamin, Shemaiah, and Jeremiah,
text.DRB: And of the children of the Priests with trumpets, Zacharias the son of Jonathan, the son of Semeia, the son of Mathania, the son of Michaia, the son Zechur, the son Asaph,

text.NRSV: and some of the young priests with trumpets: Zechariah son of Jonathan son of Shemaiah son of Mattaniah son of Micaiah son of Zaccur son of Asaph;
text.Rotherham: and, of the sons of the priests, with trumpets, Zechariah—son of Jonathan—son of Shemaiah—son of Mattaniah, son of Micaiah, son of Zaccur, son of Asaph;
text.KJV: And certain of the priests’ sons with trumpets; namely, Zechariah the son of Jonathan, the son of Shemaiah, the son of Mattaniah, the son of Michaiah, the son of Zaccur, the son of Asaph:
text.NABRE: priests with the trumpets, and also Zechariah, son of Jonathan, son of Shemaiah, son of Mattaniah, son of Micaiah, son of Zaccur, son of Asaph,
text.DRB: and his brethren Semeia, and Azareel, Malalai, Galalai, Maai, Nathanael, and Judas, and Hanani, with the instruments of the song of David the man of God: and Esdras the scribe before them in the gate of the fountain.

text.NRSV: and his kindred, Shemaiah, Azarel, Milalai, Gilalai, Maai, Nethanel, Judah, and Hanani, with the musical instruments of David the man of God; and the scribe Ezra went in front of them.
text.Rotherham: and his brethren—Shemaiah and Azarel, Milalai, Gilalai, Maai, Nethanel, and Judah, Hanani, with the instruments of music of David, the man of God,—with Ezra the scribe before them;
text.KJV: And his brethren, Shemaiah, and Azarael, Milalai, Gilalai, Maai, Nethaneel, and Judah, Hanani, with the musical instruments of David the man of God, and Ezra the scribe before them.
text.NABRE: and his kinsmen Shemaiah, Azarel, Milalai, Gilalai, Maai, Nethanel, Judah, and Hanani, with the musical instruments of David, the man of God. Ezra the scribe was at their head.
text.DRB: And against them there went up in the stairs of the city of David, in the rising of the wall above the house of David, and unto the gate of waters toward the East.

text.NRSV: At the Fountain Gate, in front of them, they went straight up by the stairs of the city of David, at the ascent of the wall, above the house of David, to the Water Gate on the east.
text.Rotherham: and, over they fountain gate and straight before them, they went up by the stairs of the city of David, at the going up of the wall,—above the house of David, even as far as the water-gate, eastward.
text.KJV: And at the fountain gate, which was over against them, they went up by the stairs of the city of David, at the going up of the wall, above the house of David, even unto the water gate eastward.
text.NABRE: At the Fountain Gate they went straight up by the steps of the City of David and continued along the top of the wall above the house of David until they came to the Water Gate on the east.
text.DRB: And the second choir of thanks-givers went on the contrary side, and I after it, & the half part of the people upon the wall, & above the tower of the furnaces, and unto the broadest wall,

text.NRSV: The other company of those who gave thanks went to the left, and I followed them with half of the people on the wall, above the Tower of the Ovens, to the Broad Wall,
text.Rotherham: And, the second choir, was going over against them, I, following it,—with the half of the people upon the wall, above the tower of the ovens, even as far as the broad wall;
text.KJV: And the other company of them that gave thanks went over against them, and I after them, and the half of the people upon the wall, from beyond the tower of the furnaces even unto the broad wall;
text.NABRE: The second choir proceeded to the left, followed by myself and the other half of the administrators, along the top of the wall past the Oven Tower as far as the Broad Wall,
text.DRB: and above the gate of Ephraim, and above the old gate, and above the gate of fishes and the tower of Hananeel, and the tower of Emath, and unto the gate of the flock: and they stood in the gate of the watch,

text.NRSV: and above the Gate of Ephraim, and by the Old Gate, and by the Fish Gate and the Tower of Hananel and the Tower of the Hundred, to the Sheep Gate; and they came to a halt at the Gate of the Guard.
text.Rotherham: and above the gate of Ephraim, and upon the old gate, and upon the fish-gate, and the tower of Hananel, and the tower of Hammeah, even as far as the sheep-gate,—and they came to a stand, at the gate of the guard.
text.KJV: And from above the gate of Ephraim, and above the old gate, and above the fish gate, and the tower of Hananeel, and the tower of Meah, even unto the sheep gate: and they stood still in the prison gate.
text.NABRE: then past the Ephraim Gate to the Mishneh Gate, the Fish Gate, the Tower of Hananel, and the Hundred Tower, as far as the Sheep Gate. They came to a halt at the Prison Gate.
text.DRB: and there stood two choirs of them that praised in the house of God, and I, and the half part of the magistrates with me.

text.NRSV: So both companies of those who gave thanks stood in the house of God, and I and half of the officials with me;
text.Rotherham: So the two choirs, came to a stand, at the house of God,—and I, and half the deputies with me;
text.KJV: So stood the two companies of them that gave thanks in the house of God, and I, and the half of the rulers with me:
text.NABRE: Both choirs took up a position in the house of God; I, too, and half the magistrates with me,
text.DRB: And the Priests, Eliachim, Maasia, Miamin, Michea, Elioenai, Zacharia, Hanania with trumpets,

text.NRSV: and the priests Eliakim, Maaseiah, Miniamin, Micaiah, Elioenai, Zechariah, and Hananiah, with trumpets;
text.Rotherham: and, the priests—Eliakim, Maaseiah, Miniamin, Micaiah, Elioenai, Zechariah, and Hananiah, with trumpets;
text.KJV: And the priests; Eliakim, Maaseiah, Miniamin, Michaiah, Elioenai, Zechariah, and Hananiah, with trumpets;
text.NABRE: together with the priests Eliakim, Maaseiah, Minjamin, Micaiah, Elioenai, Zechariah, Hananiah, with the trumpets,
text.DRB: and Maasia, & Semeia, & Eleazar, & Azzi, & Johanan, and Melchia, and Ælam, and Ezer. And the singing men sang aloud, and Iezraia the overseer:

text.NRSV: and Maaseiah, Shemaiah, Eleazar, Uzzi, Jehohanan, Malchijah, Elam, and Ezer. And the singers sang with Jezrahiah as their leader.
text.Rotherham: and Maaseiah and Shemaiah and Eleazar and Uzzi and Jehohanan and Malchijah and Elam and Ezer,—and the musicians sounded, aloud with Jezrahiah who was over them.
text.KJV: And Maaseiah, and Shemaiah, and Eleazar, and Uzzi, and Jehohanan, and Malchijah, and Elam, and Ezer. And the singers sang loud, with Jezrahiah their overseer.
text.NABRE: and Maaseiah, Shemaiah, Eleazar, Uzzi, Jehohanan, Malchijah, Elam, and Ezer. The singers were heard under the leadership of Jezrahiah.
text.DRB: and they immolated in that day great victims, and rejoiced: for God had made them joyful with great joy: yea their wives also & children rejoiced, & the joy of Jerusalem was heard far off.

text.NRSV: They offered great sacrifices that day and rejoiced, for God had made them rejoice with great joy; the women and children also rejoiced. The joy of Jerusalem was heard far away.
text.Rotherham: And they sacrificed, on that day, great sacrifices, and rejoiced, for, God, had caused them to rejoice with great joy, moreover also, the women and children, rejoiced,—so that the rejoicing of Jerusalem was heard afar off.
text.KJV: Also that day they offered great sacrifices, and rejoiced: for God had made them rejoice with great joy: the wives also and the children rejoiced: so that the joy of Jerusalem was heard even afar off.
text.NABRE: Great sacrifices were offered on that day, and they rejoiced, for God had given them cause for great rejoicing. The women and the children joined in, and the rejoicing at Jerusalem could be heard from far off.
text.DRB: They numbered also in that day men over the storehouses of the treasure, for the libaments, and for the first fruits, & for the tithes, that the Princes of the city might bring in by them in the honour of thanks-giving, Priests and Levites: because Juda was made joyful in the Priests and Levites standing by.

text.NRSV: On that day men were appointed over the chambers for the stores, the contributions, the first fruits, and the tithes, to gather into them the portions required by the law for the priests and for the Levites from the fields belonging to the towns; for Judah rejoiced over the priests and the Levites who ministered.
text.Rotherham: And there were set in charge, on that day, certain men, over the chambers for the treasures, for the heave-offerings, for the firstfruits, and for the tithes, to gather into them, out of the fields of the cities, the portions appointed by the law, for the priests, and for the Levites,—for, the joy of Judah, was over the priests and over the Levites, who were remaining.
text.KJV: And at that time were some appointed over the chambers for the treasures, for the offerings, for the firstfruits, and for the tithes, to gather into them out of the fields of the cities the portions of the law for the priests and Levites: for Judah rejoiced for the priests and for the Levites that waited.
text.NABRE: At that time men were appointed over the chambers set aside for stores, offerings, first fruits, and tithes; in them they were to collect from the fields of the various cities the portions legally assigned to the priests and Levites. For Judah rejoiced in its appointed priests and Levites
text.DRB: And they kept the watch of their God, and the observance of expiation, and the singing men, and the porters, according to the precept of David, and of Salomon his son,

text.NRSV: They performed the service of their God and the service of purification, as did the singers and the gatekeepers, according to the command of David and his son Solomon.
text.Rotherham: So they kept the charge of their God, and the charge of the purification, and [so did] the singers and the doorkeepers,—according to the commandment of David and of Solomon his son.
text.KJV: And both the singers and the porters kept the ward of their God, and the ward of the purification, according to the commandment of David, and of Solomon his son.
text.NABRE: who carried out the ministry of their God and the ministry of purification (as did the singers and the gatekeepers) in accordance with the prescriptions of David and Solomon, his son.
text.DRB: because in the days of David and Asaph from the beginning there were Princes appointed of the singing men in song praising, and confessing to God.

text.NRSV: For in the days of David and Asaph long ago there was a leader of the singers, and there were songs of praise and thanksgiving to God.
text.Rotherham: For, in the days of David and Asaph, of old, there were chiefs of the singers, and songs of praise and thanksgiving, unto God.
text.KJV: For in the days of David and Asaph of old there were chief of the singers, and songs of praise and thanksgiving unto God.
text.NABRE: For in the days of David and Asaph, long ago, there were leaders of singers for songs of praise and thanksgiving to God.
text.DRB: And all Israel, in the days of Zorobabel, and in the days of Nehemias gave portions to the singing men, and to the porters day by day, and they sanctified the Levites, and the Levites sanctified the children of Aaron.

text.NRSV: In the days of Zerubbabel and in the days of Nehemiah all Israel gave the daily portions for the singers and the gatekeepers. They set apart that which was for the Levites; and the Levites set apart that which was for the descendants of Aaron.
text.Rotherham: Now, all Israel—in the days of Zerubbabel, and in the days of Nehemiah, used to give the portions of the singers and the doorkeepers, the need of a day upon its day,—and they hallowed them unto the Levites, and, the Levites, hallowed them unto the sons of Aaron.
text.KJV: And all Israel in the days of Zerubbabel, and in the days of Nehemiah, gave the portions of the singers and the porters, every day his portion: and they sanctified holy things unto the Levites; and the Levites sanctified them unto the children of Aaron.
text.NABRE: All Israel, in the days of Zerubbabel and in the days of Nehemiah, gave the singers and the gatekeepers their portions, according to their daily needs. They made their consecrated offering to the Levites, and the Levites made theirs to the descendants of Aaron.

text.NRSV: On that day they read from the book of Moses in the hearing of the people; and in it was found written that no Ammonite or Moabite should ever enter the assembly of God,
text.Rotherham: On that day, a portion, was read in the book of Moses, in the ears of the people,—and it was found written therein, that the Ammonite and the Moabite should not enter into the convocation of God, unto times age-abiding;
text.KJV: On that day they read in the book of Moses in the audience of the people; and therein was found written, that the Ammonite and the Moabite should not come into the congregation of God for ever;
text.NABRE: Chapter 13 At that time, when the book of Moses was being read in the hearing of the people, it was found written there: “No Ammonite or Moabite may ever be admitted into the assembly of God;
text.DRB: And in that day there was read in the volume of Moyses the people hearing it: and there was found written in it, that the Ammonites and the Moabites should not enter into the Church of God for ever:

text.NRSV: because they did not meet the Israelites with bread and water, but hired Balaam against them to curse them—yet our God turned the curse into a blessing.
text.Rotherham: because they met not the sons of Israel, with bread and with water,—but hired against them Balaam, to curse them, although our God turned the curse into a blessing.
text.KJV: Because they met not the children of Israel with bread and with water, but hired Balaam against them, that he should curse them: howbeit our God turned the curse into a blessing.
text.NABRE: for they did not meet the Israelites with food and water, but they hired Balaam to curse them, though our God turned the curse into a blessing.”
text.DRB: for that they met not the children of Israel with bread and water: and they hired against them Balaam to curse them: and our God turned the cursing into blessing.

text.NRSV: When the people heard the law, they separated from Israel all those of foreign descent.
text.Rotherham: So it came to pass, when they had heard the law,—that they separated all the mixed multitude from Israel.
text.KJV: Now it came to pass, when they had heard the law, that they separated from Israel all the mixed multitude.
text.NABRE: When they had heard the law, they separated all those of mixed descent from Israel.
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when they had heard the law, they separated every stranger from Israel.

text.NRSV: Now before this, the priest Eliashib, who was appointed over the chambers of the house of our God, and who was related to Tobiah,
text.Rotherham: Now, before this, Eliashib the priest, who was set over a chamber of the house of God, was allied unto Tobiah;
text.KJV: And before this, Eliashib the priest, having the oversight of the chamber of the house of our God, was allied unto Tobiah:
text.NABRE: Reform in the Temple. Before this, the priest Eliashib, who had been placed in charge of the chambers of the house of our God and who was an associate of Tobiah,
text.DRB: And over this thing was Eliasib the Priest, who had been made overseer in the treasury of the house of our God, and near a-kin to Tobias.

text.NRSV: prepared for Tobiah a large room where they had previously put the grain offering, the frankincense, the vessels, and the tithes of grain, wine, and oil, which were given by commandment to the Levites, singers, and gatekeepers, and the contributions for the priests.
text.Rotherham: so he prepared him a large chamber, where aforetime they used to lay the meal-offering, the frankincense, and the utensils, and the tithe of the corn, the new wine and the oil, in charge of the Levites, and the singers, and the doorkeepers,—and the heave-offerings for the priests.
text.KJV: And he had prepared for him a great chamber, where aforetime they laid the meat offerings, the frankincense, and the vessels, and the tithes of the corn, the new wine, and the oil, which was commanded to be given to the Levites, and the singers, and the porters; and the offerings of the priests.
text.NABRE: had set aside for the latter’s use a large chamber in which had previously been stored the grain offerings, incense and vessels, the tithes in grain, wine, and oil allotted to the Levites, singers, and gatekeepers, and the offerings due the priests.
text.DRB: He therefore made to himself a great treasury, and they were there before him laying up gifts, and frankincense, and vessels, and the tithe of corn, of wine, and of oil, the portions of the Levites, and of the singing men, and of the porters, and the first fruits of the Priests.

text.NRSV: While this was taking place I was not in Jerusalem, for in the thirty-second year of King Artaxerxes of Babylon I went to the king. After some time I asked leave of the king
text.Rotherham: But, throughout all this [time], was I not in Jerusalem,—for, in the thirty-second year of Artaxerxes king of Babylon, I came unto the king, and, at the end of certain days, obtained I leave of the king;
text.KJV: But in all this time was not I at Jerusalem: for in the two and thirtieth year of Artaxerxes king of Babylon came I unto the king, and after certain days obtained I leave of the king:
text.NABRE: During all this time I had not been in Jerusalem, for in the thirty-second year of Artaxerxes, king of Babylon, I had gone back to the king. After a suitable period of time, however, I asked leave of the king
text.DRB: But in all these things I was not in Jerusalem, because in the two and thirtieth year of Artaxerxes the king of Babylon I came to the king, and in the end of certain days I desired the king.

text.NRSV: and returned to Jerusalem. I then discovered the wrong that Eliashib had done on behalf of Tobiah, preparing a room for him in the courts of the house of God.
text.Rotherham: and came to Jerusalem,—and had intelligence of the wickedness which Eliashib had committed for Tobiah, in preparing for him a chamber, in the courts of the house of God;
text.KJV: And I came to Jerusalem, and understood of the evil that Eliashib did for Tobiah, in preparing him a chamber in the courts of the house of God.
text.NABRE: and returned to Jerusalem, where I discovered the evil thing that Eliashib had done for Tobiah, in setting aside for him a chamber in the courts of the house of God.
text.DRB: And I came to Jerusalem, and I understood the evil that Eliasib had done to Tobias, to make him a treasure in the entrances of the house of God.

text.NRSV: And I was very angry, and I threw all the household furniture of Tobiah out of the room.
text.Rotherham: and it grieved me exceedingly,—and I cast forth all the household utensils of Tobiah, outside of the chamber.
text.KJV: And it grieved me sore: therefore I cast forth all the household stuff of Tobiah out of the chamber.
text.NABRE: This displeased me very much, so I had all of Tobiah’s household goods thrown outside the chamber.
text.DRB: And it seemed to me exceeding evil. And I threw forth the vessels of the house of Tobias out of the treasury:

text.NRSV: Then I gave orders and they cleansed the chambers, and I brought back the vessels of the house of God, with the grain offering and the frankincense.
text.Rotherham: Then commanded I, and they purified the chambers,—and I put back there, the utensils of the house of God, the meal-offering and the frankincense.
text.KJV: Then I commanded, and they cleansed the chambers: and thither brought I again the vessels of the house of God, with the meat offering and the frankincense.
text.NABRE: Then I gave orders to purify the chambers, and I brought back the vessels of the house of God, the grain offerings, and the incense.
text.DRB: and I commanded and they cleansed the treasury: and I brought thither again the vessels of the house of God, the sacrifice, and the frankincense.

text.NRSV: I also found out that the portions of the Levites had not been given to them; so that the Levites and the singers, who had conducted the service, had gone back to their fields.
text.Rotherham: Then came I to know, that, the portions of the Levites, had not been given [them],—so that the Levites and the singers, who had been doing the work, had fled every one to his field.
text.KJV: And I perceived that the portions of the Levites had not been given them: for the Levites and the singers, that did the work, were fled every one to his field.
text.NABRE: I learned, too, that the portions due the Levites were no longer being given, so that the Levites and the singers who should have been carrying out the services had deserted to their own fields.
text.DRB: And I knew that the portions of the Levites had not been given: and that every man was fled into his country of the Levites, and the singing men, and of them that ministered:

text.NRSV: So I remonstrated with the officials and said, “Why is the house of God forsaken?” And I gathered them together and set them in their stations.
text.Rotherham: Therefore contended I with the deputies, and said, Wherefore is the house of God, forsaken? So I gathered them together, and set them in their place.
text.KJV: Then contended I with the rulers, and said, Why is the house of God forsaken? And I gathered them together, and set them in their place.
text.NABRE: I reprimanded the magistrates, demanding, “Why is the house of God neglected?” Then I brought the Levites together and had them resume their stations.
text.DRB: and I pleaded the matter against the magistrates, and said: Why have we forsaken the house of God? And I assembled them, and I made them to stand in their standings.

text.NRSV: Then all Judah brought the tithe of the grain, wine, and oil into the storehouses.
text.Rotherham: Then, all Judah, brought in the tithe of corn, and new wine, and oil, unto the treasuries;
text.KJV: Then brought all Judah the tithe of the corn and the new wine and the oil unto the treasuries.
text.NABRE: All Judah once more brought in the tithes of grain, wine, and oil to the storerooms.
text.DRB: And all Juda carried the tithe of the corn, wine, and oil into the store-houses:

text.NRSV: And I appointed as treasurers over the storehouses the priest Shelemiah, the scribe Zadok, and Pedaiah of the Levites, and as their assistant Hanan son of Zaccur son of Mattaniah, for they were considered faithful; and their duty was to distribute to their associates.
text.Rotherham: and I made treasurers over the treasuries—Shelemiah the priest, and Zadok the scribe, and Pedaiah from among the Levites, and, next unto them, Hanan son of Zaccur, son of Mattaniah,—for faithful were they accounted, and it was [laid] upon them to distribute unto their brethren.
text.KJV: And I made treasurers over the treasuries, Shelemiah the priest, and Zadok the scribe, and of the Levites, Pedaiah: and next to them was Hanan the son of Zaccur, the son of Mattaniah: for they were counted faithful, and their office was to distribute unto their brethren.
text.NABRE: In charge of the storerooms I appointed Shelemiah the priest, Zadok the scribe, and Pedaiah, one of the Levites, together with Hanan, son of Zaccur, son of Mattaniah, as their assistant; for they were considered trustworthy. It was their duty to make the distribution to their kinsmen.
text.DRB: And we appointed over the store-houses Selemias Priest, and Sadoc scribe, and Phadaia of the Levites, and next to them Hanan the son of Zachur, the son of Mathania: because they were approved faithful, and to them were committed the portions of the brethren.

text.NRSV: Remember me, O my God, concerning this, and do not wipe out my good deeds that I have done for the house of my God and for his service.
text.Rotherham: Remember me, O my God, concerning this,—and do not wipe out my lovingkindnesses, which I have done for the house of my God, and for those keeping charge thereof.
text.KJV: Remember me, O my God, concerning this, and wipe not out my good deeds that I have done for the house of my God, and for the offices thereof.
text.NABRE: Remember this to my credit, my God! Do not forget the good deeds I have done for the house of my God and its services!
text.DRB: Remember me my God for this thing, and wipe not out my mercies, which I have done in the house of my God, and in his ceremonies.

text.NRSV: In those days I saw in Judah people treading wine presses on the sabbath, and bringing in heaps of grain and loading them on donkeys; and also wine, grapes, figs, and all kinds of burdens, which they brought into Jerusalem on the sabbath day; and I warned them at that time against selling food.
text.Rotherham: In those days, saw I in Judah—some treading winepresses on the sabbath, and bringing in sheaves and lading asses, moreover also wine, grapes, and figs, and every kind of burden, which they were bringing into Jerusalem on the sabbath day,—so I protested against it, as a day for them to sell provisions.
text.KJV: In those days saw I in Judah some treading wine presses on the sabbath, and bringing in sheaves, and lading asses; as also wine, grapes, and figs, and all manner of burdens, which they brought into Jerusalem on the sabbath day: and I testified against them in the day wherein they sold victuals.
text.NABRE: Sabbath Observance. In those days I perceived that people in Judah were treading the wine presses on the sabbath; that they were bringing in sheaves of grain, loading them on their donkeys, together with wine, grapes, figs, and every other kind of load, and bringing them to Jerusalem on the sabbath day. I warned them to sell none of these provisions.
text.DRB: In those days I saw them in Juda treading the presses on the Sabbath, carrying heaps, and loading upon asses wine, and grapes, and figs, and all manner of burden, and bringing it into Jerusalem on the Sabbath day. And I charged them, that they should sell on a day that it was lawful to sell in.

text.NRSV: Tyrians also, who lived in the city, brought in fish and all kinds of merchandise and sold them on the sabbath to the people of Judah, and in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, men of Tyre, dwelt therein, who were bringing in fish—and every kind of ware for sale,—and were selling, on the sabbath, to the sons of Judah, and in Jerusalem;
text.KJV: There dwelt men of Tyre also therein, which brought fish, and all manner of ware, and sold on the sabbath unto the children of Judah, and in Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: In Jerusalem itself the Tyrians residing there were importing fish and every other kind of merchandise and selling it to the Judahites on the sabbath.
text.DRB: And the Tyrians dwelt in it, bringing fishes, and all things to sell: and they sold on the Sabbaths to the children of Juda in Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Then I remonstrated with the nobles of Judah and said to them, “What is this evil thing that you are doing, profaning the sabbath day?
text.Rotherham: so I contended with the nobles of Judah,—and said unto them, What is this wicked thing which, ye, are doing, and profaning the sabbath day?
text.KJV: Then I contended with the nobles of Judah, and said unto them, What evil thing is this that ye do, and profane the sabbath day?
text.NABRE: I reprimanded the nobles of Judah, demanding: “What is this evil thing you are doing, profaning the sabbath day?
text.DRB: And I rebuked the Princes of Juda, and said to them: What is this evil thing, that you do, and profane the day of the Sabbath?

text.NRSV: Did not your ancestors act in this way, and did not our God bring all this disaster on us and on this city? Yet you bring more wrath on Israel by profaning the sabbath.”
text.Rotherham: Was it not, thus, your fathers did, and our God brought upon us all this calamity, and upon this city?—and, ye, would add indignation against Israel, by profaning the sabbath!
text.KJV: Did not your fathers thus, and did not our God bring all this evil upon us, and upon this city? yet ye bring more wrath upon Israel by profaning the sabbath.
text.NABRE: Did not your ancestors act in this same way, with the result that our God has brought all this evil upon us and upon this city? Would you add to the wrath against Israel by once more profaning the sabbath?”
text.DRB: Why, did not our fathers these things, and our God brought upon us all this evil, and upon this city? And you add wrath upon Israel in violating the Sabbath.

text.NRSV: When it began to be dark at the gates of Jerusalem before the sabbath, I commanded that the doors should be shut and gave orders that they should not be opened until after the sabbath. And I set some of my servants over the gates, to prevent any burden from being brought in on the sabbath day.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when the gates of Jerusalem made a shadow before the sabbath, then gave I word, and they shut the doors, and I gave word, that they should not open them, until after the sabbath,—and, some of my young men, set I near the gates, so that no burden should be brought in, on the sabbath day.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, that when the gates of Jerusalem began to be dark before the sabbath, I commanded that the gates should be shut, and charged that they should not be opened till after the sabbath: and some of my servants set I at the gates, that there should no burden be brought in on the sabbath day.
text.NABRE: When the shadows were falling on the gates of Jerusalem before the sabbath, I ordered the doors to be closed and prohibited their reopening until after the sabbath. I posted some of my own people at the gates so that no load might enter on the sabbath day.
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when the gates of Jerusalem had rested on the Sabbath day, I spake: and they shut the gates, and I commanded them that they should not open them till after the Sabbath: and of my servants I appointed over the gates, that none should bring in burdens in the Sabbath day.

text.NRSV: Then the merchants and sellers of all kinds of merchandise spent the night outside Jerusalem once or twice.
text.Rotherham: But the traders and sellers of all kinds of wares lodged outside Jerusalem once or twice.
text.KJV: So the merchants and sellers of all kind of ware lodged without Jerusalem once or twice.
text.NABRE: The merchants and sellers of various kinds of merchandise spent the night once or twice outside Jerusalem,
text.DRB: And the merchants, and they that sold all merchandise, tarried without Jerusalem once and again.

text.NRSV: But I warned them and said to them, “Why do you spend the night in front of the wall? If you do so again, I will lay hands on you.” From that time on they did not come on the sabbath.
text.Rotherham: Therefore I testified against them, and said unto them, Wherefore are ye lodging against the wall? if ye do it again, a hand, will I thrust upon you,—From that time, they came not on the sabbath.
text.KJV: Then I testified against them, and said unto them, Why lodge ye about the wall? if ye do so again, I will lay hands on you. From that time forth came they no more on the sabbath.
text.NABRE: but then I warned them: “Why do you spend the night alongside the wall? If you keep this up, I will beat you!” From that time on, they did not return on the sabbath.
text.DRB: And I charged them, and I said to them: Why tarry you over-against the wall? If you shall do so the second time, I will lay my hand upon you. Therefore from that time they came not on the Sabbath.

text.NRSV: And I commanded the Levites that they should purify themselves and come and guard the gates, to keep the sabbath day holy. Remember this also in my favor, O my God, and spare me according to the greatness of your steadfast love.
text.Rotherham: And I gave word to the Levites, that they should be purifying themselves and coming in, as keepers of the gates, to hallow the sabbath day. This also, remember to me, O my God, and have pity upon me, according to the abundance of thy lovingkindness.
text.KJV: And I commanded the Levites that they should cleanse themselves, and that they should come and keep the gates, to sanctify the sabbath day. Remember me, O my God, concerning this also, and spare me according to the greatness of thy mercy.
text.NABRE: Then I ordered the Levites to purify themselves and to watch the gates, so that the sabbath day might be kept holy. This, too, remember in my favor, my God, and have mercy on me in accordance with your great mercy!
text.DRB: Ispake also to the Levites that they should be cleansed, and should come to keep the gates, and to sanctify the day of the Sabbath: therefore for this also remember me my God, and spare me according to the multitude of thy mercies.

text.NRSV: In those days also I saw Jews who had married women of Ashdod, Ammon, and Moab;
text.Rotherham: Moreover, in these days, saw I the Jews who had married women of Ashdod, of Ammon, of Moab;
text.KJV: In those days also saw I Jews that had married wives of Ashdod, of Ammon, and of Moab:
text.NABRE: Mixed Marriages. Also in those days I saw Jews who had married women of Ashdod, Ammon, or Moab.
text.DRB: But in those days also I saw the Jews marrying wives women of Azotus, and of Ammon, and of Moab.

text.NRSV: and half of their children spoke the language of Ashdod, and they could not speak the language of Judah, but spoke the language of various peoples.
text.Rotherham: and, their children, were one-half speaking the language of Ashdod, and understood not how to speak the language of the Jews,—but after the tongue of both people.
text.KJV: And their children spake half in the speech of Ashdod, and could not speak in the Jews’ language, but according to the language of each people.
text.NABRE: Of their children, half spoke the language of Ashdod, or of one of the other peoples, and none of them knew how to speak the language of Judah.
text.DRB: And their children spake, the half part the Azotian tongue, and they could not speak the Jews' language, and they spake according to the language of the people and people.

text.NRSV: And I contended with them and cursed them and beat some of them and pulled out their hair; and I made them take an oath in the name of God, saying, “You shall not give your daughters to their sons, or take their daughters for your sons or for yourselves.
text.Rotherham: So I contended with them, and laid a curse upon them, and I smote, from among them, certain men, and pulled out their hair,—and I put them on oath by God, Ye shall not give your daughters unto their sons, nor take of their daughters, for your sons nor for yourselves.
text.KJV: And I contended with them, and cursed them, and smote certain of them, and plucked off their hair, and made them swear by God, saying, Ye shall not give your daughters unto their sons, nor take their daughters unto your sons, or for yourselves.
text.NABRE: So I reprimanded and cursed them; I beat some of their men and pulled out their hair; and I adjured them by God: “You shall not marry your daughters to their sons nor accept any of their daughters for your sons or for yourselves!
text.DRB: And I rebuked them, and cursed them. And I beat of them some men, & shaved them bald, & adjured them by God, that they should not give their daughters to their sons, nor take their daughters for their sons, and for themselves, saying:

text.NRSV: Did not King Solomon of Israel sin on account of such women? Among the many nations there was no king like him, and he was beloved by his God, and God made him king over all Israel; nevertheless, foreign women made even him to sin.
text.Rotherham: Was it not, over these things, that Solomon king of Israel sinned—though, among many nations, there was no king such as he, and he was, beloved by his God, and so God gave him to be king over all Israel,—even him, did foreign women, cause to sin.
text.KJV: Did not Solomon king of Israel sin by these things? yet among many nations was there no king like him, who was beloved of his God, and God made him king over all Israel: nevertheless even him did outlandish women cause to sin.
text.NABRE: Did not Solomon, the king of Israel, sin because of them? Though among the many nations there was no king like him, and though he was beloved of his God and God had made him king over all Israel, yet even he was led into sin by foreign women.
text.DRB: Did not Salomon the king of Israel sin in this kind of thing? And surely in many nations, there was not a king like to him, and he was beloved of his God, and God set him king over all Israel: him therefore also foreign women brought to sin.

text.NRSV: Shall we then listen to you and do all this great evil and act treacherously against our God by marrying foreign women?”
text.Rotherham: Unto you, then, shall we hearken, to do all this great wickedness, to act unfaithfully with our God,—by marrying foreign wives?
text.KJV: Shall we then hearken unto you to do all this great evil, to transgress against our God in marrying strange wives?
text.NABRE: Must it also be heard of you that you have done this same terrible evil, betraying our God by marrying foreign women?”
text.DRB: And shall we also being disobedient persons do all this great evil, to transgress against our God, and to marry foreign women?

text.NRSV: And one of the sons of Jehoiada, son of the high priest Eliashib, was the son-in-law of Sanballat the Horonite; I chased him away from me.
text.Rotherham: And, one of the sons of Joiada, son of Eliashib the high priest, was son-in-law to Sanballat the Horonite,—therefore I chased him from me.
text.KJV: And one of the sons of Joiada, the son of Eliashib the high priest, was son in law to Sanballat the Horonite: therefore I chased him from me.
text.NABRE: One of the sons of Joiada, son of Eliashib the high priest, was the son-in-law of Sanballat the Horonite! I drove him from my presence.
text.DRB: And Sanaballat the Horonite was son in law to one of the sons of Joiada, the son of Eliasib the high Priest, whom I drave from me.

text.NRSV: Remember them, O my God, because they have defiled the priesthood, the covenant of the priests and the Levites.
text.Rotherham: Remember them, O my God,—on account of the Defilings of the Priesthood, and the Covenant of the Priesthood, and of the Levites.
text.KJV: Remember them, O my God, because they have defiled the priesthood, and the covenant of the priesthood, and of the Levites.
text.NABRE: Remember against them, my God, how they defiled the priesthood and the covenant of the priesthood and the Levites!
text.DRB: Remember o Lord my God against them, that pollute the Priesthood, and the right of Priests and Levites.

text.NRSV: Thus I cleansed them from everything foreign, and I established the duties of the priests and Levites, each in his work;
text.Rotherham: So I purified them from everything foreign,—and appointed charges unto the priests and unto the Levites, every one in his work;
text.KJV: Thus cleansed I them from all strangers, and appointed the wards of the priests and the Levites, every one in his business;
text.NABRE: So I cleansed them of all foreign contamination. I established the various functions for the priests and Levites, so that each had an appointed task.
text.DRB: Therefore I separated from them all strangers, & I appointed the courses of the Priests and Levites, every man in his ministry:

text.NRSV: and I provided for the wood offering, at appointed times, and for the first fruits. Remember me, O my God, for good.
text.Rotherham: also for the offering of wood, at times appointed, and for the firstfruits. Remember me, O my God, for good!
text.KJV: And for the wood offering, at times appointed, and for the firstfruits. Remember me, O my God, for good.
text.NABRE: I also provided for the procurement of wood at stated times and for the first fruits. Remember this in my favor, my God!
text.DRB: and in the oblation of wood at times appointed, and in the first fruits: Remember me my God unto good. Amen.

4.19: for I had said to the nobles, the magistrates, and the rest of the people: “Our work is scattered and extensive, and we are widely separated from one another along the wall;
4.20: wherever you hear the trumpet sound, join us there; our God will fight with us.”
4.21: Thus we went on with the work, half with spears in hand, from daybreak till the stars came out.
4.22: At the same time I told the people to spend the nights inside Jerusalem, each with an attendant, so that they might serve as a guard by night and a working force by day.
4.23: Neither I, nor my kindred, nor any of my attendants, nor any of the bodyguard that accompanied me took off our clothes; everyone kept a weapon at hand.
